subject: Sociology
title: Ethnic Enclaves
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Ethnic Enclaves.

hook.text: Walk ten blocks in New York and you cross Chinatown, Little Italy, and Harlem. Cities are mosaics of ethnic worlds.

buildup.text: Ethnic enclaves are neighborhoods where a single group concentrates, maintaining language, food, and social networks.

discovery.text: Enclaves offer newcomers jobs, housing, and community without requiring fluency in the dominant language.

twist.text: Critics say enclaves slow assimilation. Research shows they also protect immigrants from exploitation and isolation.

climax.text: Enclaves aren't traps. They're launchpads. Many second-generation immigrants leave by choice after building resources.

punchline.text: Enclaves don't isolate people. They give them a place to start.

quiz.question: What role do ethnic enclaves play for immigrants?
options: They provide jobs, community, and protection
They permanently isolate immigrants from society
They are created by government relocation programs
